description: Rust port of psych::fa and FactoMineR::FAMD: exploratory factor analysis on numeric data, factor analysis of mixed data, and a standalone correlation-matrix builder covering the full psych::fa(cor=...) method surface.
release_notes:
Initial release. Rust port of `psych::fa` and `FactoMineR::FAMD` with three `ocp-json/1` commands:

- `fa`: EFA on numeric data. Iterative principal-axis / minimum-residual factor extraction (SMC communality init, fallback to max-abs-off-diag on singular R), Kaiser-normalized analytic Varimax rotation (pairwise column rotation matching `stats::varimax`), per-variable rows sorted by dominant factor then |loading|. Iterates over meta `batch` groupings Ã— `[min_count, max_count]` factors.
- `famd`: Factor analysis of mixed data. Standardises numerics (n-1 SD), disjunctively codes categoricals with `(indicator - proportion)/sqrt(proportion)`, thin SVD of the concatenated `X/sqrt(n)`. Emits coord / contrib / cosÂ² per dimension at both variable and level granularity.
- `correlation`: Standalone correlation-matrix builder. Accepts the full `psych::fa(cor=...)` method enum; Phase 1 implements `pearson` and `covariance`. Writes a CSV plus a three-sheet xlsx (correlation, n_obs, meta).

Phase 1 scope: `pearson` / `covariance` correlations, `minres` / `pa` extraction, `none` / `varimax` rotation, full FAMD. Other cor/fm/rotate values round-trip through validation and return `NotImplemented` at runtime pending Phases 2-3 (categorical correlations; remaining factoring methods and GPArotation rotations).
